reforms.description: The Canada Apprentice Loan was proposed and implemented to provide apprentices registered in their first Red Seal trade with interest-free loans of up to CAD 4,000 per period of technical training, aimed at reducing financial barriers to completing in-school training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing financial barriers, the reform encourages more apprentices to complete their training, thereby enhancing the skilled labor force.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was announced in the 2014 federal budget and has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While it is a significant reform aimed at improving access to training, it does not represent a structural change in the system.
reforms.source_quote: To reduce the financial barrier to apprentices completing their in-school training, the interest-free Canada Apprentice Loan of up to CAD 4000 per period of technical training for apprentices in their first Red Seal trade was proposed in the 2014 federal budget.

reforms.description: The Canadian government improved the targeting of government support for business R&D by adjusting the SR&ED tax subsidies and increasing direct support for business innovation, making nearly CAD 1.9 billion available over four years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increased funding and improved targeting are expected to enhance business innovation and R&D activities, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was implemented as part of the 2014 budget adjustments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the funding mechanisms for business R&D, significantly increasing available resources.
reforms.source_quote: Budget 2012 announced changes to simplify, enhance predictability and improve the cost-effectiveness of the SR&ED programme.

reforms.description: The Venture Capital Action Plan (VCAP) was announced to deploy CAD 400 million in new capital to strengthen Canada's venture capital industry and increase risk financing available to innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the availability of venture capital, the plan aims to support the growth of innovative firms, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The VCAP represents a significant investment in the venture capital sector, which is crucial for supporting startups and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Government of Canada announced in January 2013 the Venture Capital Action Plan (VCAP), a comprehensive strategy for deploying CAD400 million in new capital.

reforms.description: The Business Innovation Access Program was launched to connect small and medium-sized enterprises with universities and research institutions to facilitate the commercialization of ideas and products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating connections between SMEs and research institutions, the program aims to enhance the commercialization of innovative ideas, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was launched and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While it is a significant initiative, it does not represent a major structural change in the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2014, the federal government launched the Business Innovation Access Program, to connect small and medium-sized enterprises with universities.

reforms.description: The Canada Job Grant aims to align public training expenditures with labor market demand by allowing employers to participate in training decisions, ensuring better job matching.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning training with labor market needs, the grant is expected to improve job matching and enhance the skills of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The grant has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards demand-driven training, which is crucial for addressing skills gaps in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: The new Canada Job Grant is intended to orient public training expenditures towards meeting labor market demand, which would improve job matching.

reforms.description: The Express Entry System was announced to improve the responsiveness of Canada's immigration system to labor market needs by allowing candidates to express interest and be ranked based on their qualifications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the immigration system to better match labor market needs, the reform is expected to enhance the availability of skilled labor.
reforms.status_evidence: The system was announced and is set to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the immigration system, aimed at addressing labor market shortages.
reforms.source_quote: To make the programmes more responsive to current labour-market conditions, in 2015 the federal government will introduce the Express Entry System.
